title: lippia repens
intro: Lippia repens, also known as bushveld tea or wild rosemary, is a shrubby plant native to southern Africa, characterized by its small, fragrant leaves and pink or white flowers. Traditionally, the leaves and stems of Lippia repens have been used for their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Lippia repens include reducing fever, relieving respiratory issues, and aiding in digestion, as well as its potential to help manage diabetes and hypertension. Lippia repens can be used in various herbal preparations, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to reap its medicinal benefits.
